title: Word Document Import Delivery
summary: Add a narrow Mammoth DOCX adapter to ordinary Docs Import while preserving current HTML conversion, media publication, write, and rebuild ownership.
content_html:
Word Document Import Delivery

Outcome

A Docs Viewer manager can select a staged .docx, inspect the same canonical-Markdown preview used by other ordinary source formats, and create a new document through the existing Docs Import service. Mammoth contributes semantic HTML and conversion messages only. Current HTML conversion, supported embedded-image publication, fresh identity allocation, document planning, source creation, and rebuilds keep their existing owners.

Delivery Boundary

In scope:

  • pin Python Mammoth 1.12.0 in requirements.txt;
  • register .docx as the stable docx ordinary source format without changing its separate eligibility as staged file media;
  • add one local-service DOCX adapter that reads the confined direct-child source in binary mode and passes Mammoth's HTML fragment into the existing HTML-content preview;
  • define one code-owned Mammoth style map for the V1 Word semantics proven by WDI-1, with external file access and embedded document style maps explicitly disabled;
  • merge useful Mammoth messages into the existing import warning list;
  • pass embedded PNG, JPEG, GIF, and WebP through the current inline-raster media planning and publication boundary;
  • prove listing, preview, repeated create, media-before-source ordering, failure behavior, and the existing browser/service contract with focused evidence;
  • close out the feature in its durable operator, architecture, dependency, parent, and roadmap owners.

Out of scope:

  • Mammoth Markdown output, Pandoc, Word-exported HTML cleanup, post-processing or rewriting Mammoth's generated HTML, or a second Markdown converter;
  • user-supplied, embedded, or document-specific style maps, broad document transforms, style inference, or conversion profiles;
  • comments, tracked changes, equations, Word metadata, footnote-specific product behavior, or fidelity repair;
  • unsupported embedded image conversion, image optimisation, or a second media pipeline;
  • a DOCX-specific modal, client-side conversion, remote upload surface, conversion job, process isolation, or timeout machinery;
  • round-trip editing or preservation of Word layout, fonts, spacing, pagination, headers, or footers.

Settled Decisions

  • Call mammoth.convert_to_html() on the validated binary source and feed result.value unchanged into generate_html_content_import_preview(); do not call Mammoth's deprecated Markdown converter or repair its generated HTML afterward.
  • Pass external_file_access=False and include_embedded_style_map=False explicitly. WDI-1 defines and proves one code-owned Mammoth style map from representative Word semantics; the style map, not an HTML rewrite, owns Word-to-HTML interpretation.
  • Keep source_format: docx visible through listing, preview, activity, and result payloads. The browser already consumes ordinary source formats generically and should not gain a DOCX workflow branch.
  • Keep the deliberate Word semantics in Word Import Style Mapping. The adapter may inspect its title marker but must not rewrite Mammoth's HTML.
  • Preserve meaningful Mammoth warning and error messages as plain warning strings without exposing physical paths or internal conversion objects.
  • Publish only the raster MIME types already supported by inline media planning. An unsupported or unreadable embedded image must produce a warning and must not leave a raw data URL in canonical Markdown.
  • Keep Mammoth's generated HTML and base64 image bodies inside the local management service. Preview/result payloads return the canonical Markdown preview, warnings, safe summaries, and media-plan metadata, but not those intermediate bodies.
  • A malformed DOCX, failed conversion, invalid Markdown preview, or failed media publication must prevent the source write. Existing media-before-source ordering remains authoritative.

Delivery Tracker

Current state: shipped. All checkpoints are complete.

Next checkpoint: none.

[x] WDI-1 — Dependency, Source Registration, And Preview Adapter

  • [x] Pin Mammoth 1.12.0 and extend the dependency import check.
  • [x] Add a DOCX staged-suffix constant, include it in ordinary document-source eligibility, and map it to source_format: docx without changing file-media ownership.
  • [x] Add the focused Mammoth adapter and explicit DOCX preview dispatch through the existing HTML-content preview.
  • [x] Record conversion of representative Title, Subtitle, heading, no-title, and unknown-style documents, then define the complete V1 code-owned Mammoth style map before implementing individual cases.
  • [x] Prove the adapter reads the mapped title marker and passes Mammoth's generated HTML unchanged into the existing HTML-content preview, without turning the first section heading into the document title.
  • [x] Normalize Mammoth messages into the current warning list and return safe conversion failures through the existing service error boundary.
  • [x] Prove direct-child confinement, symlink rejection, DOCX listing, Title and heading behavior, semantic prose/lists/links/tables, title fallback, warning propagation, and malformed-package failure with focused tests.

WDI-1 pins and verifies Mammoth, registers .docx alongside its existing file-media eligibility, and implements the semantics recorded in Word Import Style Mapping. The adapter inspects semantic markers but passes Mammoth's generated HTML unchanged into the existing HTML-content preview. The focused DOCX, listing, source-format, HTML, media-package, and ordinary import set passes 52 tests.

[x] WDI-2 — Embedded Media And Managed Apply

  • [x] Accept only current supported embedded raster MIME types and reuse inline-raster planning, retargeting, publication, and verification.
  • [x] Preserve apply-time access to the planned image bytes without echoing generated HTML or base64 bodies to the browser.
  • [x] Warn and safely omit unsupported or unreadable embedded images rather than retaining raw data URLs.
  • [x] Prove preview-only behavior, create, repeated create, media-before-source ordering, rebuild delegation, and no source write after conversion or publication failure.
  • [x] Confirm the existing generic import UI presents DOCX selection, warnings, results, and multi-selection without a new format-specific branch; add browser coverage only if a durable module/route contract changes.

WDI-2 gives Mammoth one adapter-owned image converter for PNG, JPEG, GIF, and WebP. It converts supported bytes to temporary data URLs for the existing inline-raster planner, omits unsupported, unreadable, or empty images with warnings, and retains the raw apply source only in local service memory. Preview and result payloads contain canonical Markdown and safe plans but no generated HTML or base64 bodies. The adjacent Docs Import set passes 56 tests, and the generic multi-selection browser smoke includes DOCX without a format-specific branch.

[x] WDI-3 — Representative Proof And Closeout

  • [x] Run at least one representative Word-authored document through preview and managed import in an isolated writable projects root.
  • [x] Verify canonical Markdown for ordinary prose, semantic headings, lists, links, a simple table, warnings, and one supported embedded image.
  • [x] Run the focused Docs Import contract tests, dependency import check, Python compilation for changed services, and git diff --check.
  • [x] Update Docs Import, Docs Import Architecture, and Dependencies with shipped behavior only.
  • [x] Mark the feature, delivery, and roadmap shipped only after the complete ordinary import path is verified.

WDI-3 previewed and imported the Word-authored A controlled document - version 1.docx into an isolated external-local Notes scope. Ordinary prose, Title, Subtitle, Heading 1 through 4, emphasis, unordered and ordered lists, a link, and a table became canonical Markdown. Word stored both the original JPG and the source HEIC as JPEG package parts; both published images byte-matched those parts, and document plus search rebuilds completed with zero warnings. The representative document exposed two focused gaps before apply: missing Word alt text now receives deterministic fallback labels in the Mammoth image adapter, and the shared HTML mapping now separates table-cell blocks while honestly flattening nested lists because Markdown tables cannot contain block lists. The final adjacent set passes 57 tests alongside compilation, dependency imports at Mammoth 1.12.0, pip check, git diff --check, and the generic DOCX multi-selection browser smoke.

Done When

  • .docx is a first-class ordinary Docs Import source while remaining independently eligible for staged file-media publication;
  • Mammoth is a small, pinned, local-service semantic-HTML adapter with external files and embedded style maps disabled;
  • existing HTML-to-Markdown, inline-raster media, ImportContent, source-create, and rebuild contracts remain authoritative;
  • supported representative content becomes valid canonical Markdown and useful conversion warnings remain visible;
  • unsupported structures and images degrade without unsafe links, raw image bodies, hidden filesystem access, or partial source writes;
  • no DOCX-specific browser workflow, fidelity engine, or parallel mutation path is introduced.
